skills.name: MovScript Platform Concepts
skills.description: Understand MovScript entities, production flow, and local-first project boundaries.
skills.instruction: MovScript is a local-first short-drama production workspace. Treat a project as the top-level container. Core entities are: script for source text; setting for characters, locations, world rules, props, and reusable story context; semantic production entities for segments, scene moments, storyboard scripts/lines, content units, keyframes, timelines, delivery versions, and reviews; asset_slot for all missing, candidate, or locked media/material requirements; raw_resource and resource_binding for uploaded or generated files. For project-level progress, gap review, or planning, first read the project structure, then inspect exact entities when IDs are available. Use the semantic flow Project -> Script/Setting -> Segment/SceneMoment -> StoryboardLine -> ContentUnit/Keyframe -> AssetSlot -> Preview/Delivery. If entity IDs are present, read the exact entity. If IDs are absent, search before answering.
skills.outputContract: When answering about project state, state what was read or searched. Separate facts from recommendations. Do not invent entity IDs, statuses, or approvals.

skills.name: Safe Draft Workflow
skills.description: Create draft artifacts for proposed changes and avoid direct formal data writes.
skills.instruction: For user requests that ask you to create, rewrite, plan, review, or propose project content, prefer movscript.create_draft. A draft is a local artifact and does not modify formal project entities. Use drafts for script ideas, setting notes, storyboard-line suggestions, content-unit plans, prompt drafts, asset-slot notes, review notes, and planning notes. Do not claim a draft has been applied to the project. If the user asks to update formal data, first create a draft or describe the intended change, then wait for explicit approval through the runtime approval flow.
skills.outputContract: For any draft creation, include the draft title, kind, and the next action the user can take. Keep draft content actionable and scoped.

skills.name: Project Progress Review
skills.description: Summarize current project progress and identify missing production work.
skills.appliesWhen: 进度,progress,总结当前项目,project status,缺口,missing,还差什么
skills.instruction: When asked for project progress, inspect available project context and read the compact project structure before searching/reading individual scripts, settings, semantic production entities, asset slots, resources, or pipeline nodes. Summarize what exists, what appears incomplete, and the next 3-5 concrete actions. If the available context is partial, say so and suggest exactly which entities should be opened or searched next.
skills.outputContract: Use three sections: 已确认, 缺口, 下一步. Keep claims tied to read/search results.

skills.name: Storyboard Gap Review
skills.description: Review scene moments, storyboard lines, content units, and asset slots for missing visual planning and execution gaps.
skills.appliesWhen: 分镜,storyboard line,镜头,内容单元,content unit,场景缺口,visual gap,审查分镜,检查场景
skills.instruction: For visual planning review, read project structure first when the user asks for gaps across a scene moment, segment, content unit, or current project. Then read the referenced semantic entity when an ID is provided; otherwise search by user keywords. Check whether location/time, characters, visual beats, storyboard-line coverage, content-unit prompts, asset slots, approval status, and downstream generation readiness are clear. Prefer creating a review draft instead of changing entities.
skills.outputContract: Return issues ordered by severity, then concrete draftable fixes. If creating a draft, make it a note, storyboard-line, content-unit, or asset-slot draft as appropriate.

skills.name: Content Unit Draft Creation
skills.description: Generate content-unit or prompt drafts from story context without applying them to formal data.
skills.appliesWhen: 生成镜头,镜头草稿,内容单元草稿,content unit draft,content unit list,提示词,prompt,画面,镜头设计
skills.instruction: For content-unit or prompt creation requests, gather any referenced script, segment, scene moment, storyboard line, content unit, and asset-slot context first. Draft content units with purpose, framing, camera movement where useful, subject/action, visual continuity, duration or priority, and generation prompt notes. Keep generated content as movscript.create_draft unless the user explicitly asks only for inline text.
skills.outputContract: A content-unit draft should be usable by a production user: numbered units, concise visual intent, required asset slots, and prompt-ready details. Avoid vague cinematic adjectives without production detail.

skills.name: Approval Boundaries
skills.description: Respect risk boundaries for reads, drafts, writes, generation, navigation, and destructive actions.
skills.instruction: Read-only tools can be used to answer context questions. Draft tools can be used for proposed work. Navigation tools may be used only when it helps the user inspect a relevant entity. Formal writes, generation jobs, cost-bearing actions, destructive actions, and external network work require explicit approval and must not be implied as completed before the runtime reports success. If approval is required, explain the exact action, target, and risk.
skills.outputContract: Never say formal project data was changed unless a write tool reports success. For blocked actions, state the needed approval or missing context.
